garutyunov.com · workflow

Ideas ship as pet projects, on rails.

A GitHub Project board feeds two delivery loops — human-gated and autonomous. Each task becomes a spec, a PR, and a live project with its own subdomain.

Project board HITL & auto loops OpenSpec Subdomains Social images
garutyunov.com

One board, two loops

Every pet project starts as an issue on a single Project board. A Loop field routes each task to one of two delivery loops that pull the next Ready task and drive it end-to-end — the only difference is where a human stands in the way.

hitl — human in the loop

Waits for the owner to approve a spec before any code is written, and leaves every PR for human review and merge. Nothing ships unattended.

auto — unattended

Skips both gates: decomposes epics, opens PRs, and self-merges once CI is green, then moves the task to Done. For work you trust the loop to finish alone.

The path of a task

1

Pick up a Ready task

The loop reads the board, reads the comments (owner direction wins), and moves the task to In progress.

2

Branch, worktree, open a PR early

Each task gets its own git worktree off fresh origin/<default>, and a PR opens immediately so CI and review have a home from the start.

3

Spec or implement

Serious work is written up as an OpenSpec change and reviewed before code; contained changes are implemented directly.

4

Ship & publish

The work lands, and the project gets the full treatment: metadata, an icon, a subdomain, and a social image.

Every project gets the full treatment

Skills apply the same finishing touches to every pet project, so they all look and behave like part of one family.

Metadata

Repo description, the pet-project topic, SPEC.md, README, and Pages config.

Icon

A minimalist 3D voxel app icon, white subject on black, generated per project.

Subdomain

Published at <name>.garutyunov.com with Cloudflare DNS + GitHub Pages.

Social image

A deterministic 1200×630 OG card screenshotted from the project's hero — no AI. This page's card was made this way.

UI kit

The shared @gaarutyunov/ui-kit design system for a consistent house style.

Specs

OpenSpec changes capture the intent and acceptance criteria before code.

Built and driven from the workspace repo · garutyunov.com
